channel.title: Cariboo Signals – TTS Preview
channel.description: Azure Neural TTS A/B test feed – temporary, this week only.
channel.itunes:author: Riley and Casey

item.title: Arts, Culture And Digital Storytelling [Azure TTS]
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. premier’s office says DRIPA changes will not be introduced this spring after all and [100 Mile Free Press] Cancer patient donates saddle sale to 100 Mile House oncology department, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can rural Cariboo communities build lasting digital storytelling capacity that preserves cultural memory while maintaining sovereignty over their own narratives? Topics include: Digitization infrastructure vs. storytelling capacity building, Cultural sovereignty and governance in digital archiving, Interpretive labor and the role of elders in meaning-making, Technology accessibility for rural communities, Institutional partnerships and power dynamics in cultural preservation.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Cariboo Chilcotin Partners for Literacy

Sources discussed:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. premier’s office says DRIPA cha...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] Cancer patient donates saddle sale to ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Canada’s alcohol relief ‘step in the...
  • CBC: [CBC] Williams Lake, Quesnel and 100 Mile House firefighters...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] New power for B.C. city’s mayor to r...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Regional district, B.C in stalemate ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Vancouver Island WildFire Whisky pai...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Prescribed burns set for north ea...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] Benefits of home-ground flour shared a...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] Some of the buzziest names in superintell...
  • Boing Boing: [Boing Boing] Turn shoebox memories into digital keepsakes w...

Additional sources provided:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Surge in demand overwhelms West Koot...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Okanagan Humane Society: An affectio...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. police watchdog probes Quesnel ...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Hackaday Podcast Episode 366: DOOM on a Toaster, ...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: Azure Neural TTS (Ava · Andrew)
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling.

Topics include: Digitization infrastructure vs. storytelling capacity building, Cultural sovereignty and governance in digital archiving, Interpretive labor and the role of elders in meaning-making, Technology accessibility for rural communities, Institutional partnerships and power dynamics in cultural preservation.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).
